prompt:
Ticket: shared task-runner library for our release scripts.

Every service repo has a bash script reimplementing dependency ordering for its generate/build/test/package steps, and they all get it subtly wrong. Build a reusable library in package taskrunner (module go-taskrunner, root of this repo) that takes a static task list and runs a target plus its transitive deps.

Required API:
- type Task struct { Name string; Deps []string; Action func(context.Context) error }
- type Status string with StatusOK = "ok", StatusFailed = "failed", StatusSkipped = "skipped"
- type Report struct { Statuses map[string]Status }
- NewRunner(tasks []Task) (*Runner, error) — reject duplicate/empty names, nil actions, deps on unknown tasks, and dependency cycles (cycle errors must contain the word "cycle"); the other validation errors should name the offending task or missing dep.
- (*Runner).Run(ctx context.Context, target string) (*Report, error)

Run semantics we need:
- only the target's transitive closure runs; unrelated tasks are never invoked and stay out of the report
- a task starts only after ALL of its deps returned nil; independent ready tasks run concurrently (goroutine each, don't serialize them)
- if an action errors: its dependents are marked skipped (transitively) and never run, but branches that don't depend on it still complete; Run returns an error that wraps the action's error (errors.Is must see it) and names the failed task
- ctx cancellation stops launching new tasks and makes Run return an error; actions receive the same ctx
- the Report comes back non-nil even on failure/cancel, with a status for every task in the closure; a Runner must be reusable for repeated Runs
- Run on a target that doesn't exist → error naming it

The acceptance contract is taskrunner_test.go — read it, don't edit it. Split the implementation into a few focused files (validation/graph/scheduling), not one giant blob. Green bar = go test -race -timeout 30s ./...
